about: The brand primitives that lynox paints on more than one surface: this product UI,
and the lynox.ai marketing site. Same brand, different codebases — so the values
are necessarily duplicated. This file is the contract that keeps the duplication
honest, and it is the ONLY thing that can: the site is a standalone Astro app and
must not depend on this package (that would drag a Svelte UI into a marketing site
and couple its build to core releases).
If you change one of these values in app.css, `pnpm tokens:contract` (from the repo
root) fails — here, in this repo, on your commit. That is the point. Update this file
in the same change, and know that the other surface has to follow.
DARK ONLY. The light ramps are deliberately NOT shared: the product is a tool you
sit in for hours (flat, quiet, neutral-grey ink); the site is scanned in twenty
seconds (tonal depth, violet-tinted ink). Two named contexts, not a bug.
This file names no other repository and no private path. It is a list of colours.

notGuarded.lyx-bg-overlay: Modal/backdrop scrim. Pure #000 here, near-black ink on light. The other surface has no equivalent — it scrims with its own tonal grounds.
notGuarded.lyx-accent-disabled: Derived, not declared: color-mix(var(--lyx-accent), var(--lyx-bg-muted)). It follows the two tokens above it automatically, so there is nothing to keep in sync.
notGuarded.lyx-focus-ring: The other surface has no focus-ring token. Nothing to converge onto yet.

alsoNotShared: The LIGHT ramps of every token above — see DARK ONLY in `about`.

The semantic SURFACES on the other surface (success/error fg+bg+border triplets, still hard-coded there). Only the three standalone FILL colours — success/danger/warning — are shared, and they are in `primitives`.
